title: The Dot Product
concept: The dot product multiplies matching elements of two vectors and adds them up, acting as a weighted sum of features.

exercises.prompt: Recap: in a standard data matrix, what does each row represent?
exercises.explain: Right — rows are examples, columns are features. Next, how does the model multiply features by weights to get a prediction?

cards.title: Calculating a grocery bill
cards.body: You buy 3 apples ($2 each) and 2 bananas ($1 each). The total bill is (3 × 2) + (2 × 1) = $8. You just did a dot product in your head!

cards.title: The dot product
cards.body: The dot product multiplies matching elements of two vectors and adds the results together. It takes two vectors of the same length and outputs a single number.

cards.title: A weighted balance
cards.body: Think of the dot product as a weighted score. One vector holds your features (size, bedrooms) and the other holds their importance (weights). The dot product combines them into a single score.

cards.title: Estimating house value
cards.body: A house vector is [2000, 3] (size, bedrooms). The weight vector is [100, 10000] (each sq ft is worth $100, each bedroom is $10k). The dot product is (2000 × 100) + (3 × 10000) = $230,000.

cards.title: Requires equal lengths
cards.body: You can't take the dot product of vectors with different lengths. If you have 3 features but only 2 weights, the math breaks down because one feature has nothing to multiply by!

cards.title: The calculation
cards.body: How the dot product is computed:

exercises.prompt: How do you calculate the dot product of two vectors?

options.text: Multiply matching elements and add the results up
Multiply all elements of both vectors together
Add matching elements and multiply the sum
Divide the first vector by the second

exercises.explain: Multiply index 1 by index 1, index 2 by index 2, and so on, then sum all those products.

exercises.prompt: True or false: the dot product of two vectors of length 3 is another vector of length 3.
exercises.explain: False — the dot product compresses two vectors into a single number (a scalar).

exercises.sentence: The dot product of `[1, 2]` and `[3, 4]` is (1 × 3) + (2 × 4) = ___.
exercises.explain: Multiply matching elements (1×3 = 3 and 2×4 = 8) and add them (3+8 = 11).

exercises.prompt: A model calculates a user preference score using a features vector and a weights vector. What operation does it use?
exercises.explain: The dot product calculates a single weighted score from features and weights.

exercises.prompt: A model predicts if a customer will buy a product. The customer vector is [2, 30] (clicks, age). The weight vector is [5, -0.1]. What is the dot product score?
exercises.explain: Outstanding! (2 × 5) + (30 × -0.1) = 10 - 3 = 7. A positive score means they're likely to buy! Next: how to do this for a whole matrix at once.
